author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …Let them “lift up their eyes to the hills whence cometh their help” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, The hills have been lightened?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The San of righteousness hath already risen, the Gospel hath been already preached by the Apostles, the Scriptures have been preached, all the mysteries have been laid open, the veil hath been rent, the secret place of the temple hath been revealed: let them now at length lift their eyes up to the hills, whence their help cometh…“Of His fulness have all we received,” he saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy help therefore is from Him, of whose fulness the hills received, not from the hills; towards which, nevertheless, save thou lift thine eyes through the Scriptures, thou wilt not approach, so as to be lighted by Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sing therefore what followeth; if thou wish to hear how thou mayest most securely set thy feet on the steps, so that thou mayest not be fatigued in that ascent, nor stumble and fall: pray in these words: “Suffer not my foot to be moved!” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whereby are feet moved; whereby was the foot of him who was in Paradise moved?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But first consider whereby the feet of him who was among the Angels were moved: who when his feet were moved fell, and from an Angel became a devil: for when his feet were moved he fell.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Seek whereby he fell: he fell through pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nothing then moveth the feet, save pride: nothing moveth the feet to a fall, save pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Charity moveth them to walk and to improve and to ascend; pride moveth them to fall…Rightly therefore the Psalmist, hearing how he may ascend and may not fall, prayeth unto God that he may profit from the vale of misery, and may not fail in the swelling of pride, in these words, “Suffer not my feet to be moved!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He replieth unto him, “Let him that keepeth thee not sleep.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend, my beloved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is as if one thought were expressed in two sentences; the man while ascending and singing “the song of degrees,” saith, “Suffer not my foot to be moved:” and it is as if God answered, Thou sayest unto Me, Let not my feet be moved: say also, “Let Him that keepeth thee not sleep,” and thy foot shall not be moved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Choose for thyself Him, who will neither sleep nor slumber, and thy foot shall not be moved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God is never asleep: if thou dost wish to have a keeper who never sleepeth, choose God for thy keeper.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Suffer not my feet to be moved,” thou sayest: well, very well: but He also saith unto thee, “Let not him that keepeth thee slumber.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou perhaps wast about to turn thyself unto men as thy keepers, and to say, whom shall I find who will not sleep? what man will not slumber? whom do I find? whither shall I go? whither shall I return?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Psalmist telleth thee: “He that keepeth Israel, shall neither slumber nor sleep” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to have a keeper who neither slumbereth nor sleepeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, “He that keepeth Israel shall neither slumber nor sleep:” for Christ keepeth Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be thou then Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth Israel?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is interpreted, Seeing God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how is God seen?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First by faith: afterwards by sight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou canst not as yet see Him by sight, see Him by faith…Who is there, who will neither slumber nor sleep? when thou seekest among men, thou art deceived; thou wilt never find one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Trust not then in any man: every man slumbereth, and will sleep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When doth he slumber?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When he beareth the flesh of weakness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When will he sleep?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When he is dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Trust not then in man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A mortal may slumber, he sleepeth in death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Seek not a keeper among men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who, thou askest, shall help me, save He who slumbereth not, nor sleepeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear what followeth: “The Lord Himself is thy keeper” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not therefore man, that slumbereth and sleepeth, but the Lord, that keepeth thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How doth He keep thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is thy defence upon the hand of thy right hand.”…It seemeth to me to have a hidden sense: otherwise he would have simply said, without qualification, “The Lord will keep thee,” without adding, “on thy right hand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth God keep our right hand, and not our left?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did He not create the whole of us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did not He who made our right hand, make our left hand also?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Finally, if it pleased Him to speak of the right hand alone, why said He, “on the hand of thy right hand,” and not at once “upon thy right hand”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why should He say this, unless He were keeping somewhat here hidden for us to arrive at by knocking?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He would either say, “The Lord shall keep thee,” and add no more; or if He would add the right hand, “The Lord shall keep thee upon thy right hand;” or at least, as He added “hand,” He would say, “The Lord shall keep thee upon thy hand, even thy right hand,” not “upon the hand of thy right hand.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I ask you, how ye interpret what is said in the Gospel, “Let not your left hand know what your right hand doeth”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if ye understand this, ye will discover what is your right hand, and what is your left: at the same time ye will also understand that God made both hands, the left and the right; yet the left ought not to know what the right doeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By our left hand is meant all that we have in a temporal way; by our right hand is meant, whatever our Lord promiseth us that is immutable and eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if He who will give everlasting life, Himself also consoleth our present life by these temporal blessings, He hath Himself made our right hand and our left…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us now come to this verse of the Psalm: “The Lord is thy defence upon the hand of thy right hand” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By hand he meaneth power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How do we prove this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because the power of God also is styled the hand of God…Whereof John saith, “He gave unto them power to become the sons of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence hast thou received this power?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To them,” he saith, “that believe in His Name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then thou believest, this very power is given thee, to be among the sons of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But to be among the sons of God, is to belong to the right hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy faith therefore is the hand of thy right hand: that is, the power that is given thee, to be among the sons of God, is the hand of thy right hand…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “May the Lord shield thee upon the hand of thy right hand” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have said, and I believe ye have recognised it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For had ye not recognised it, and that from the Scriptures, ye would not signify your understanding of it by your voices.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since then ye have understood, brethren, consider what followeth; wherefore the Lord shieldeth thee “upon the hand of thy right hand,” that is, in thy faith, wherein we have received “power to become the sons of God,” and to be on His right hand: wherefore should God shield us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On account of offences.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence come offences?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Offences are to be feared from two quarters, for there are two precepts upon which the whole Law hangeth and the Prophets, the love of God and of our neighbour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church is loved for the sake of our neighbour, but God for the sake of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of God, is understood the sun figuratively: of the Church, is understood the moon figuratively.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoever can err, so as to think otherwise of God than he ought, believing not the Father and the Son and the Holy Ghost to be of one Substance, has been deceived by the cunning of heretics, chiefly of the Arians.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he hath believed anything less in the Son or in the Holy Spirit than in the Father, he hath suffered an offence in God; he is scorched by the sun.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoever again believeth that the Church existeth in one province only, and not that she is diffused over the whole world, and whoso believeth them that say, “Lo here,” and “Lo there, is Christ,” as ye but now heard when the Gospel was being read; since He who gave so great a price, purchased the whole world: he is offended, so to speak, in his neighbour, and is burnt by the moon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoever therefore erreth in the very Substance of Truth, is burnt by the sun, and is burnt through the day; because he erreth in Wisdom itself…God therefore hath made one sun, which riseth upon the good and the evil, that sun which the good and the evil see; but that Sun is another one, not created, not born, through whom all things were made; where is the intelligence of the Immutable Truth: of this the ungodly say, “the Sun rose not upon us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whosoever erreth not in Wisdom itself, is not burnt by the sun.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whosoever erreth not in the Church, and in the Lord’s Flesh, and in those things which were done for us in time, is not burnt by the moon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But every man although he believeth in Christ, erreth either in this or that respect, unless what is here prayed for, “The Lord is thy defence upon the hand of thy right hand,” is realized in him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He goeth on to say, “So that the sun shall not burn thee by day, nor the moon by night” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy defence, therefore, is upon the hand of thy right hand for this reason, that the sun may not burn thee by day, nor the moon by night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Understand hence, brethren, that it is spoken figuratively.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, in truth, if we think of the visible sun, it burneth by day: doth the moon burn by night?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is burning?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle’s words: “Who is weak, and I am not weak? who is offended, and I burn not?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Lord shall preserve thee from all evil” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From offences in the sun, from offences in the moon, from all evil shall He preserve thee, who is thy defence upon the hand of thy right hand, who will not sleep nor slumber.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And for what reason?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because we are amid temptations: “The Lord shall preserve thee from all evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord preserve thy soul:” even thy very soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord preserve thy going out and thy coming in, from this time forth for evermore” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not thy body; for the Martyrs were consumed in the body: but “the Lord preserve thy soul;” for the Martyrs yielded not up their souls.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The persecutors raged against Crispina, whose birthday we are to-day celebrating; they were raging against a rich and delicate woman: but she was strong, for the Lord was her defence upon the hand of her right hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was her Keeper.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is there any one in Africa, my brethren, who knoweth her not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For she was most illustrious, noble in birth, abounding in wealth: but all these things were in her left hand, beneath her head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: An enemy advanced to strike her head, and the left hand was presented to him, which was under her head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Her head was above, the right hand embraced her from above.…
